title: DD Triple-Interaction: WB Tertiary Enrollment + Granger & Reverse-Causality Tests
layman_summary: Replaces a frozen education proxy with World Bank tertiary enrollment, re-runs a bias-corrected panel regression, and checks the results against alternative causal explanations using Granger tests.
summary: This experiment implements the Giesselmann-Schmidt-Catran (2022) Double-Demeaning (DD) panel estimator for a triple interaction (Education × Gini × SocProt → Liberal Democracy) using World Bank gross tertiary enrollment as a replacement for the frozen UNDP MYS proxy. Key findings: (1) WB tertiary enrollment has 140.8× more within-country variation than the frozen UNDP MYS (within-SD 3.66 vs 0.026), resolving the near-zero-variation artifact that limited prior iterations. (2) Five regression specifications compared — Spec A (UNDP naive, β₇=0.0198, p=0.195, N=161), Spec B (UNDP DD, β₇=155.23, p=0.195, N=161; ill-conditioned due to 2-period panel), Spec C (WB tertiary DD, β₇=13.13, p=0.189, N=137; z-score fallback + OLS), Spec D (WB tertiary naive, β₇=0.0019, p=0.189, N=137), Spec E (lagged SocProt, β₇=0, p=nan, N=51; insufficient after lag). (3) Panel Granger causality: Education→LDem significant (p=0.005), Gini→LDem not significant (p=0.328), consistent with causal direction. (4) Reverse-causality checks: LDem→Gini path not significant (p=0.708, reassuring), SAP→SocProt borderline (p=0.154). (5) Marginal effect grid computed for 2×2 (Gini p25/p75) × (SocProt p25/p75) combinations. All results serialized to full_method_out.json (13 examples in exp_gen_sol_out schema). Schema validation passed. Mini and preview variants generated. Pyproject.toml updated with pinned dependency versions.
